# Style Reference Guide

<!-- Generated by design-style-agent -->
<!-- Project: Task Management Tool for Small Teams -->
<!-- Basis: Tailwind CSS 4 + Shadcn UI baseline with blue-slate internal-tool overrides -->

---

## 1. Typography

**Fonts:** Use the Geist Sans variable font (already wired as `--font-sans` in the `@theme` block). No custom font import is needed.

**Heading hierarchy:**
- `h1` (`text-2xl font-semibold`): Page titles only — e.g., "Tasks", "Task Detail". One per page.
- `h2` (`text-lg font-medium`): Section headings within a page — e.g., "Task Information", "Due Date".
- `h3` (`text-base font-medium`): Sub-section labels — use sparingly.
- Avoid `h4`–`h6` in this application; favour label + value pairs inside `<Card>` instead.

**Body text:** `text-sm` (14 px) for list rows, form labels, and card content. `text-base` (16 px) for page-level descriptions or onboarding copy.

**Labels and captions:** `text-xs text-muted-foreground` for helper text beneath form inputs and timestamp displays (e.g., "Due: 3 Jun 2026").

**Font weights:** `font-medium` for column headers and emphasis; `font-semibold` for primary actions and page titles; `font-normal` for all body content.

**Line length:** Keep prose inside `<Card>` content to a maximum of `max-w-prose` (65 ch). Task description fields should be `max-w-prose` wide.

---

## 2. Spacing

Use Tailwind's default 4 px base scale throughout. Key conventions:

| Context | Utility |
|---|---|
| Page outer padding (mobile) | `px-4 py-6` |
| Page outer padding (tablet+) | `px-6 py-8` or `px-8 py-8` |
| Card internal padding | `p-6` (Shadcn `<Card>` default; do not override) |
| Section gap between cards or blocks | `space-y-6` or `gap-6` in a grid |
| Form field vertical spacing | `space-y-4` between `<FormField>` groups |
| Inline icon-to-label gap | `gap-2` |
| Button horizontal padding | Shadcn default (`px-4`) — do not override |
| Table/list row height | Shadcn `<TableRow>` default (do not override) |

Avoid custom `margin` or `padding` values outside the Tailwind scale. If a design requires it, round to the nearest scale value.

---

## 3. Component Selection

**When to use `<Card>`:** All task list items beyond simple rows. Task Detail screen body. Form sections. Empty-state containers. Prefer `<Card>` over bare `<div>` for any visually grouped content block.

**When to use `<Dialog>`:** Delete Confirmation (R11–R13). Create Task form (R7) — implement as a modal `<Dialog>` rather than a separate page unless the form becomes too complex. Keep dialog content to a maximum of two visible scroll-lengths.

**When NOT to use `<Dialog>`:** Task Detail (R6) — use a dedicated route/page, not a dialog, so the URL is shareable and browser back works.

**Task List:** Use Shadcn `<Table>` (`<TableHeader>`, `<TableBody>`, `<TableRow>`, `<TableCell>`) for the task list on tablet and desktop. On mobile (`< 768 px`), switch to a stacked `<Card>` list (one card per task). Implement this with a responsive breakpoint, not two separate components.

**Status badge:** Use Shadcn `<Badge>` with `variant="secondary"` for `pending` status and `variant="default"` (primary blue) for `complete` status. Do not use custom coloured `<div>` elements.

**Role-gated controls:** Admin-only buttons (Create, Edit due date, Delete) must be conditionally rendered — do not use `disabled` to hide role-restricted actions, use conditional render so they are absent from the DOM for Member users.

**Form inputs:** Use Shadcn `<Form>` + `<FormField>` + `<Input>` / `<Select>` / `<Textarea>` / `<DatePicker>`. Do not build custom input wrappers.

**Confirm dialogs:** Use a single reusable `<ConfirmDialog>` component (wrapping Shadcn `<Dialog>`) for the Delete Confirmation. It receives title, description, and onConfirm/onCancel props.

**Loading states:** Use Shadcn `<Skeleton>` for data-fetching placeholders in the task list and task detail. Do not use spinner-only states — skeleton layouts reduce layout shift.

**Error states:** Use Shadcn `<Alert variant="destructive">` for inline async errors (R — NFR5). Include a retry `<Button variant="outline">` beneath the alert.

---

## 4. Layout

**Max content width:** `max-w-5xl mx-auto` for the main content area on desktop. On tablet, `max-w-3xl`.

**Page structure:**
```
<AppShell>          ← persistent nav/sidebar + top bar
  <main>
    <PageHeader />  ← h1 + optional primary action button (right-aligned)
    <ContentArea /> ← task list, detail, or form
  </main>
</AppShell>
```

**Navigation:** Use a top navigation bar (not a sidebar) for this application. The nav bar contains the app name/logo (left), the current user's name (right), and a sign-out button. No sidebar is needed for this feature set.

**Task List layout:**
- Desktop (`≥ 1280 px`): Full `<Table>` with columns: Title, Assigned To (Admin only), Due Date, Status, Actions.
- Tablet (`768–1279 px`): Same table, but "Assigned To" column hidden (Admin sees it via task detail).
- Mobile (`< 768 px`): Stacked `<Card>` list. Each card shows title, due date chip, and status badge.

**Create Task form:** Center the `<Dialog>` on screen. Form width: `max-w-lg` inside the dialog.

**Task Detail:** Two-column layout on desktop (task info left, metadata/actions right). Single column on mobile. Use `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6`; info takes `col-span-2`, metadata takes `col-span-1`.

**Responsive breakpoints (per NFR3):**
- Mobile: `≥ 360 px`
- Tablet: `≥ 768 px` (Tailwind `md:`)
- Desktop: `≥ 1280 px` (Tailwind `xl:`)

---

## 5. Motion and Animation

**Principle:** Subtle and purposeful. Transitions should confirm state changes without drawing attention to themselves.

**Default transition:** `transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out` on interactive elements (buttons, links, table rows). Do not add extra animation beyond what Shadcn components provide out of the box.

**Dialog open/close:** Use the `modalEnter` / `modalExit` keyframes already defined in `globals.css`. Apply via Shadcn's `<Dialog>` built-in animation — do not override.

**Status change (mark complete):** Brief `transition-colors` on the `<Badge>` when task status changes from `pending` to `complete`. No bounce or pulse effect.

**Loading skeletons:** No animation overrides — use Shadcn `<Skeleton>` default pulse.

**Reduced motion:** Already handled globally in `globals.css` via `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`. Do not add `will-change` or `transform` on elements that don't need it. Respect reduced-motion in any custom JS-triggered animations.

---

## 6. Accessibility

**WCAG 2.1 Level AA is required** (NFR1). Specific requirements:

**Contrast ratios:**
- Body text on background: `--foreground` on `--background` = near-black on white — well above 7:1 (AAA).
- Primary button text: `--primary-foreground` (white) on `--primary` (blue-slate `oklch(0.42 0.105 250)`) — verify at approximately 4.7:1 (AA pass). Use a contrast checker before shipping.
- Muted text (`--muted-foreground`) on `--background`: the chosen oklch value (~52% lightness) yields approximately 4.6:1 on white — borderline AA. Use `text-muted-foreground` only for supplementary text (captions, helper text), never for primary readable content.
- Badge text: Use Shadcn badge defaults. If you apply custom `bg-*` colours on badges, re-verify contrast.

**Focus rings:** All interactive elements inherit `outline-ring/50` from `@layer base`. Do not suppress focus rings with `focus:outline-none` unless you replace them with `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`.

**Form labels:** Every `<Input>`, `<Select>`, and `<Textarea>` must have a visible `<Label>` via Shadcn `<FormLabel>`. Do not rely on placeholder text as the only label.

**Keyboard navigation:**
- Tab order must follow visual reading order.
- `<Dialog>` must trap focus while open — Shadcn `<Dialog>` does this by default; do not override.
- Delete Confirmation dialog must move focus to the "Cancel" button on open (safer default).
- Task list rows must be keyboard-activatable via Enter/Space when rendered as links or buttons.

**ARIA:**
- Status badges for task `status` should include `aria-label="Status: pending"` or `aria-label="Status: complete"`.
- Loading skeletons should have `aria-busy="true"` on their container.
- Empty state containers should use `role="status"` with a descriptive accessible name.

**Images and icons:** All decorative icons must have `aria-hidden="true"`. Functional icons (used as the sole affordance for an action) must have an `aria-label` on their wrapping button.

---

## 7. Brand Tone

**Voice:** Professional and direct. This is an internal productivity tool; copy should be clear and action-oriented, not playful or conversational.

**Button labels:**
- Use verb phrases: "Create Task", "Save Changes", "Mark Complete", "Delete Task", "Cancel".
- Avoid ambiguous labels like "OK", "Yes", "Submit" in isolation — pair them with context: "Confirm Delete", "Save Changes".
- Destructive action button (in Delete Confirmation) must be labelled "Delete Task", styled with `variant="destructive"`.

**Error messages:**
- Be specific about what failed: "Title is required." not "Something went wrong."
- Validation errors: present inline beneath the field using Shadcn `<FormMessage>`.
- Async errors: present in `<Alert variant="destructive">` above the relevant content with a retry button.
- Do not use exclamation marks in error messages.

**Empty states:**
- Admin empty state (no tasks in system): "No tasks yet. Create the first task to get started." + "Create Task" `<Button>`.
- Member empty state (no tasks assigned): "You have no tasks assigned right now."
- Keep empty state copy neutral — do not imply the user did something wrong.

**Confirmation dialogs:**
- Delete Confirmation dialog title: "Delete Task"
- Body: "This action cannot be undone. The task \"[task title]\" will be permanently removed."
- Buttons: "Cancel" (left, `variant="outline"`) | "Delete Task" (right, `variant="destructive"`).

**Form placeholder text:** Use short prompts: "Enter task title", "Add a description (optional)". Placeholders are not labels — always pair with a visible `<FormLabel>`.

**POPIA compliance copy (CR1, CR4):**
- Login screen must include a purpose statement and a link to the privacy policy.
- Suggested login-screen helper text: "We collect your name and email address to manage task assignments. See our [Privacy Policy](#) for details."
- Link text for the privacy policy must be descriptive: "Privacy Policy" — never "click here".

---

*End of Style Reference Guide*
